sources.name: Internal Usage Metrics (IUM)
sources.plainEnglish: Monthly tenant-level product usage and outcome metrics. This is the cleanest source for adoption and headline KPI trends when an IUM already exists.
sources.freshness: Live monthly feed. Current recruiting dashboards generally resolve to SANDBOX for the latest visible month in the accessible warehouse path.
usefulDataPoints: Average Time to Hire (2358, latest visible month currently SANDBOX)
Number of Offers accepted. (2346, live-resolved by name)
Employment Agreement Acceptance (2360, live-resolved by name)
Count of Internal Job Applications last month (2349, live-resolved by name)
Applicant gender, race/ethnicity, and age-band counts (multiple live-resolved metrics)
Legacy Time to Fill snapshot from the older 2359 mapping (keep as historical reference until a fresh live name match is proven)
Add Documents configured / used / created (1757-1760 in current verified mapping)
pmNotes: Best when you need adoption or outcome trends by month, tenant, region proxy, or infrastructure.
Always cast `value` to numeric and note whether the dashboard uses SANDBOX or PROD.
Metric meanings can drift by ID over time, so verify the metric name before reusing an old query.

Live discovery on 12 April 2026 found recruiting-oriented IUM rows for offers accepted, employment agreement acceptance, internal job applications, applicant demographic counts, and recruiter productivity. Resolve these by metric_name first before assuming older ID mappings are still valid.
Older workspace assumptions that 2360 meant positions with open requisitions and 2361 meant positions filled are stale in the current live warehouse.

Fresh query checks on 12 April 2026 found the latest visible month for the live-resolved recruiting metrics used here under SANDBOX only; no parallel PROD rows surfaced for that same current slice in the accessible table.

The older Time to Fill dashboard still relies on a historical 2359 extract, but current live discovery also surfaces 2359 on applicant age-band metrics, so do not reuse 2359 for new work without metric-name revalidation.

sources.name: Workday Usage Metrics (WUM)
sources.plainEnglish: Tenant-level Workday usage metrics with metric_id, metric_name, return_type (including Boolean), and monthly buckets. Used for organisation-level feature adoption (for example Uses Recruiting, Uses Onboarding) alongside IUM.
sources.freshness: Live monthly feed; filter wd_event_date and wd_env_type (PROD for production adoption).
usefulDataPoints: Boolean feature adoption per tenant (return_type = Boolean)
metric_id + metric_name pairs for Recruiting / HCM areas
usedInDashboards: Recruiting adoption trend (aggregate series in design/data-recruiting-adoption.ts)
pmNotes: Large table: always include wd_event_date partition predicate and narrow year/month when joining at tenant grain.
Boolean values arrive as varchar; cast carefully and treat missing rows as unknown, not false.
Superseded by PCA task-to-feature mapping + customer360 usage for Customer Scorecard feature adoption (April 2026).

sources.name: Customer Monthly Feature Usage
sources.plainEnglish: Per-tenant monthly feature adoption flags across all Workday product areas. 200 tenants, 198 features, 16 months (2024-01 to 2025-04). Each row is a tenant + feature + month with adopted = 0 or 1.
sources.freshness: Monthly grain; latest available month is 2025-04 (approximately one year old). The _test suffix suggests this may be an experimental table.
usefulDataPoints: Per-tenant feature adoption (adopted = 0/1) for 198 distinct features
Feature names spanning Recruiting, HCM, Financials, Payroll, Student, and more
Recruiting-specific features: Find Candidates, Recruiting Hub, Recruiting Landing Pages, Source Requisition, Hire BP Consolidation, etc.
pmNotes: Directly queryable from Pharos CLI (confirmed working).
Superseded on Customer Scorecard by PCA mappings (customer360 + task_to_pca_mapping) as of April 2026 for better Recruiting/TA feature coverage (~5,908 tenants vs ~200).
Retains validated status as fallback when PCA mappings are unavailable or for broad cross-product feature flags.
Only ~200 tenants vs ~5,908 in PCA approach.

sources.name: Customer360 Worklet Adoption
sources.plainEnglish: Tenant-task-month usage table for worklets and UI tasks, with product and product_area taxonomies plus usage counts. Core source for Worklet Adoption Analytics.
sources.freshness: Historical through 2026-03 in this workspace; PROD-only rows observed.
pmNotes: Partition filters are required on wd_event_date and wd_env_type.
Superset semantic layer may alias measure names (for example total_ons_transactions) while physical table uses total_oms_transactions.
Join to task_to_pca_mapping on task_id to convert task usage into PCA feature adoption.
For correlation scoring, align this usage window with IUM outcome windows and remove aggregate pseudo-tenants (for example '- All Tenants').
Used in Customer Scorecard for feature adoption correlation with TTH/TTF. Segment filters (region, industry) sourced from interview_dashboard_tenant_filters.
TTF metric (2359) requeried with >0 filter on 11 Apr 2026; only ~981 tenants report positive TTF values.

sources.name: Task to PCA Feature Mapping
sources.plainEnglish: Mapping bridge from task_id to PCA metadata (feature, capability, entitlement, XO product area/product, module), used to define Product Catalog-aligned adoption.
sources.freshness: Current snapshot table; not time-series on its own.
usefulDataPoints: entitlement labels including Recruiting and Recruiting - LDP
usedInDashboards: Customer Scorecard (PCA source of truth for TA feature list)
pmNotes: Treat this as the canonical feature dictionary for Recruiting/Talent Acquisition.
Use with customer360 usage to compute enabled vs not enabled by tenant.
Feature coverage improves materially vs coarse WUM booleans or small curated flags.
Correlation guidance is non-causal: score by effect size + support + adjusted significance and always show caveats.

sources.name: Business Process Event Stats
sources.plainEnglish: One row per business process event with pre-computed duration and status. This is the primary source for sub-BP cycle time, completion, cancellation, and quality metrics.
sources.freshness: Live through today. Use PROD for real customer behaviour unless there is a clear reason not to.
usefulDataPoints: Completed average and median cycle time
Sent back / corrected / reassigned quality indicators
usedInDashboards: Job App Sub-BPs / BP Durations dashboard
BP Durations dashboard segmented views (region / industry / tenant) — same rows joined to interview_dashboard_tenant_filters at generation time
pmNotes: Use this before `bp_event_record_stats` if the question is about whole-process performance.
Filter by status when analysing duration, or cancelled and in-progress work will distort the result.
Segmented slices in the UI are pre-materialised: tenant_n matches tenant_name in dw.user_test.interview_dashboard_tenant_filters for region and super_industry.

sources.name: Business Process Step Records
sources.plainEnglish: Very large step-level event data across Workday business processes. Use it when you need task names, task durations, or step-level volumes that do not exist in the event-level table.
sources.freshness: Live through today, but expensive to query. Narrow the date range and task filters.
usefulDataPoints: Unique tenant coverage for a task
usedInDashboards: Offer step discovery for future dashboard additions
BP Durations Offer tab — Offer steps, approvals, bottleneck analyser (design/data-offer-steps.ts)
pmNotes: Confirmed Offer step names include `Generate Document`, `Review Documents`, `Review Writer Generated Document`, `Add Documents`, and `Manage Attachments`.

Approval tasks charted after exact-name lock (PROD, high volume): `Bulk Approve` and `Approve Business Process (Web Service)` — Web Service path has very low distinct-tenant coverage; interpret with care.
We have not yet validated a clean `Initiate Offer` task in this table, so do not chart it yet.
Because the table is huge, exploratory regex searches can take minutes and should be followed by narrower exact-name queries.

Trino requires a partition predicate on `wd_event_date`; combine with `CAST(creation_time AS date)` bounds when you need to align months to dashboard LABELS and avoid grouping long-backdated rows into recent months.

sources.name: OMS Request Logs
sources.plainEnglish: Detailed request logs for OMS tasks, including display names, durations, and request payload snippets. Useful for understanding what the application actually called when a user clicked through a workflow.
sources.freshness: Live log stream. Best for targeted discovery rather than broad trend dashboards.
usefulDataPoints: Potential attachment or document payload fields
usedInDashboards: No production dashboard yet - currently used for Offer step and attachment discovery
pmNotes: Good for proving task semantics before you put a metric on a dashboard.
Potential source for candidate attachment analysis via `processed_element_json` and `oms_request_json`, but we have not validated a stable attachment-type field yet.

sources.name: Document Repository Events
sources.plainEnglish: Repository-level document events with a document kind, reference id, and latency. Helpful when the question is about document storage or repository activity rather than the business process itself.
sources.freshness: Live log stream.
usefulDataPoints: Reference ids for possible correlation work
usedInDashboards: No production dashboard yet - reviewed for Offer attachment research
pmNotes: Current top `document_kind` values are operational document-system categories rather than clean candidate attachment types.
Treat this as a supporting source until we can prove a reliable join back to Offer tasks or attachment categories.

sources.name: Interview Initiation and Decision Events
sources.plainEnglish: Live interview-related event data for job applications and interview rounds. This is the main source for interview volume and time-in-process analysis.
sources.freshness: Live through yesterday.
pmNotes: Prefer this over older snapshot tables for active interview analysis.

sources.name: Interview Scheduling Events
sources.plainEnglish: Live scheduling task events for interview flows, including Schedule Interview and Schedule Interview Team.
sources.freshness: Live through yesterday.
usefulDataPoints: Segmentation by company size, region, and industry
pmNotes: Useful when the PM question is specifically about scheduling friction rather than the whole Interview BP.

sources.name: Interviewer Engagement
sources.plainEnglish: Rater and interviewer activity data, including ratings submitted and non-rating tasks.
sources.freshness: Live through yesterday.
pmNotes: Best for diagnosing interviewer participation rather than recruiter workflow speed.

sources.name: MISST Interaction Events
sources.plainEnglish: Page interaction events for Manage Interview Scheduling Settings. Useful for measuring how much effort recruiters spend inside MISST.
sources.freshness: Live through yesterday.
usefulDataPoints: Average total MISST time per requisition
pmNotes: Useful for productivity questions and aligns closely with the equivalent Superset view.

sources.name: Interview Dashboard Tenant Filters
sources.plainEnglish: Tenant-level segmentation and feature-adoption lookup table used to enrich metrics with company size, region, industry, and feature flags.
sources.freshness: Static lookup / slower-moving support table.
pmNotes: Use as enrichment join for region (segment) and industry (super_industry) per tenant.
Used by Customer Scorecard for industry/region segment filters, peer benchmarking, and top-10 enrichment.
Used by BP Duration dashboard for segment breakdown filters.
Original interview flag columns are no longer used on the Customer Scorecard (replaced by PCA-based adoption from customer360 + task_to_pca_mapping).
6 regions (APAC, Corporate, EMEA, Japan, North America, US Federal), 14 industries. ~225 tenants have missing values.

sources.name: Interview Session Enrichment Stats
sources.plainEnglish: Pre-aggregated interview session counts, including whether sessions used competencies or questionnaires.
sources.freshness: Continuously updated.
pmNotes: Helpful when the PM wants a clean count metric without re-deriving enrichment joins.

sources.name: Career Hub Job Events
sources.plainEnglish: Career Hub interaction events for job browsing and apply-click behaviour. Useful for directional reach and application-start proxies when packaged career-site funnel metrics are unavailable.
sources.freshness: Live event data; partition filter on wd_event_date is required.
usefulDataPoints: Distinct tenants and users interacting with job cards
pmNotes: Live discovery on 12 April 2026 confirmed high-volume impression and apply events.
Good candidate for directional funnel work, especially reach and apply-start proxies.
Not yet validated as the tracker definition for external career site reach, starts, or submitted applications.

sources.name: Employee Retention Study
sources.plainEnglish: Tenant-level employee retention study table with retention rates plus recruiting and product-usage flags.
sources.freshness: Queryable now; likely a study or analytic output rather than a canonical warehouse fact table.
usefulDataPoints: tenant segment, industry, and enterprise size
pmNotes: Live discovery on 12 April 2026 confirmed the table is accessible in Pharos.
This is adjacent to retention analysis, but it does not match the tracker definition of recruiting-sourced new hires retained after 12 months.
Treat as a research input or proxy, not a drop-in replacement for the blocked new-hire retention metric.

sources.name: BP Job Application Event Records
sources.plainEnglish: Step-level business-process event records tied to job applications, including top_event_id, bp_type_id, task_name, and timing fields.
sources.freshness: Queryable now; expensive enough to need tight filters.
usefulDataPoints: action_event_id and top_event_id for chain reconstruction
pmNotes: Promising for future event-chain work on job-application semantics.
Live discovery on 12 April 2026 did not surface clean offer / employment rows under obvious filters, so it is not yet a reliable source for issued or renegotiated Offer/EA counts.
Use only for targeted exploration until a reproducible semantic mapping is proven.

sources.name: Mixology Task Telemetry
sources.plainEnglish: UX telemetry for specific workflows and tasks, including initiate-offer, compensation-change, and hire-employee flows.
sources.freshness: Live UX telemetry; requires partition filter on wd_event_date.
pmNotes: Live discovery on 12 April 2026 confirmed these tables are accessible.
Sample rows looked like generic page telemetry rather than PM-ready business-event facts.
Do not use these as a substitute for Offer issued, renegotiation, or JR Agent time metrics without additional semantic validation.

sources.name: Peakon-Adjacent Aggregate Table
sources.plainEnglish: Aggregate monthly table with total usage and country / segment distribution fields. The name suggests a Peakon link, but the exposed fields look like adoption aggregates rather than employee engagement scores.
sources.freshness: Queryable now in Pharos.
pmNotes: Live discovery on 12 April 2026 confirmed the table exists and is queryable.
Schema does not expose new-hire score, sentiment, or respondent-level Peakon outcomes.
Not a validated source for the tracker metric 'Peakon new-hire scores'.
